description: §41.15.8 — schemaFor(User) called in an ATTRIBUTE-VALUE interpolation position (a <div title=${ schemaFor(User) }>). This is 'any other markup context' outside a <schema> block, so it SHALL fire E-SCHEMAFOR-INVALID-CALL-CONTEXT. Regression guard: the invalid-context walker (Pass B) previously inspected only its EXPR_FIELDS whitelist + children/body and never attr/handler expression nodes, so the call compiled clean and leaked a runtime schemaFor() call (compile-only; throws at runtime) into client.js.
rationale: §41.15.8 normative statement: schemaFor(...) calls outside a <schema> block — including inside any other markup context — SHALL emit E-SCHEMAFOR-INVALID-CALL-CONTEXT. The function-call form is canonical INSIDE <schema> blocks only (its output is a table-declaration fragment that requires the <schema> parser context). An attribute-value interpolation is one such invalid markup context. Resolution: wrap the call inside a <schema>${ schemaFor(...) }</> block.
